wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite present/future form — it means 'will watch' but this sentence is about yesterday.
wrong_choice_explanations.ません: ません is polite negative — it means 'don't watch' but this sentence is about completing an action.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't watch' but this sentence is positive.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite present/future form — it means 'will eat' but this sentence is about completed action.
wrong_choice_explanations.た: た is the plain past form — it's too casual for this polite sentence structure.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't eat' but this sentence is positive.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite present/future form — it means 'will talk' but this sentence is about past action.
wrong_choice_explanations.ません: ません is polite negative — it means 'don't talk' but this sentence describes completed action.
wrong_choice_explanations.た: た is the plain past form — it's too casual for this polite sentence structure.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite present/future form — it means 'will do' but this sentence is about completed homework.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't do' but this sentence is about completing homework.
wrong_choice_explanations.てる: てる is a contraction of ている (ongoing action) — it doesn't fit this past completion context.

exercises.translation: I read a book at the library.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite present/future form — it means 'will read' but this sentence is about past action.
wrong_choice_explanations.ません: ません is polite negative — it means 'don't read' but this sentence describes completed reading.
wrong_choice_explanations.だ: だ is a plain copula — it doesn't work with verb stems and is wrong grammatically here.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite present/future form — it means 'will buy' but this sentence is about completed purchase.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't buy' but this sentence is positive.
wrong_choice_explanations.ている: ている indicates ongoing action or state — it doesn't fit this completed past action context.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite present/future form — it means 'will listen' but this sentence is about past action.
wrong_choice_explanations.ません: ません is polite negative — it means 'don't listen' but this sentence describes completed listening.
wrong_choice_explanations.なかった: なかった is plain past negative — it's too casual for this polite sentence and also negative.

exercises.translation: I took a walk in the park.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite present/future form — it means 'will walk' but this sentence is about past action.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't walk' but this sentence is positive.
wrong_choice_explanations.ない: ない is plain negative present — it doesn't match the past tense or polite level needed here.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite present/future form — it means 'will call' but this sentence is about completed call.
wrong_choice_explanations.ません: ません is polite negative — it means 'don't call' but this sentence describes completed action.
wrong_choice_explanations.でした: でした is the past form of です — it can't attach directly to verb stems like かけ.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite present/future form — it means 'will study' but this sentence is about past action.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't study' but this sentence is positive.
wrong_choice_explanations.ていた: ていた indicates past ongoing action — while grammatically possible, ました is the standard polite past form needed here.
